page: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine belonging to the EPDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by 614 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the middle tier for most multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 28904.71, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 9470.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1127.57 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 122.17 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance represents a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scoring in the bottom 25% at 1087.0, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also performing weakly at 8.49 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is also relatively low, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 2590.42.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the inclusion of 614 GB of local storage, this instance offers a resource-dense profile suited for memory-intensive workloads and database operations. PassMark database operations scored 10869.44, and Redis SET operations reached 21.74 million operations per second, both falling within the average tier. The server is suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-optimized web serving, though it is less optimal for single-threaded compute tasks or heavy cryptographic processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the EPDSv5 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and includes 614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28904.71 and Redis SET operations at 21.74 million operations per second. However, single-core capabilities are a tradeoff, as shown by a weak Geekbench single-core score of 1087.0 and lower single-threaded decompression speeds. This memory-optimized configuration is suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and local storage without requiring high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, suited for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v5 is an ARM64 VM with 16 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for database and memory-intensive workloads.

128 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance with 28904.71 stress-ng score
Tradeoff in single-core performance and cryptographic speeds
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
